name: Historic loop from Mauléon-Licharre
name_original: Boucle historique à partir de Mauléon-Licharre
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mauléon-Licharre traverse the diverse landscapes of the Soule province in the French Basque Country. The region features a mix of hills, green countryside, and the Saison River, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Elevated positions offer views of the Saison Valley and the distant Pyrenees mountains. The area is characterized by its natural beauty, with routes passing through charming villages and mountainous areas. Best touring cycling routes around Mauléon-Licharre The most popular touring cycling route is Big loop starting from Mauléon-Licharre, a 51.1 miles (82.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the regional landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bridge over the Saison River – Uhaïtxa loop from Mauléon-Licharre, a difficult 54.3 miles (87.4 km) path. This route includes challenging climbs and views of the Saison River. Local touring cyclists also love the Ahusquy Inn – Bridge over the Saison River loop from Mauléon-Licharre, a 34.2 miles (55.0 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Mauléon-Licharre is defined by rolling hills, the Saison Valley, and distant views of the Pyrenees. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Mauléon-Licharre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mauléon-Licharre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mauléon-Licharre?
A: There are nearly 30 touring cycling routes around Mauléon-Licharre listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in Mauléon-Licharre?
A: The terrain around Mauléon-Licharre is characterized by rolling hills, the scenic Saison Valley, and distant views of the Pyrenees. Routes often traverse green countryside and charming villages, with some segments potentially unpaved. You can expect significant elevation changes on many routes.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Mauléon-Licharre offers many challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e861825129/big-loop-starting-from-mauleon-licharre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Big loop starting from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 82.3 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41078739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Saison River – Uhaïtxa loop from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt;, which is 87.4 km long and includes challenging climbs.

Q: What are the best times of year to go touring cycling in Mauléon-Licharre?
A: May and July are particularly popular months for cycling in the region, suggesting favorable weather conditions. The diverse landscapes are enjoyable throughout the warmer seasons, offering pleasant rides through green countryside.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy stunning natural beauty, including panoramic views of the Saison Valley and the distant Pyrenees mountains. Routes often pass through lush green countryside and offer glimpses of the Saison River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Osquich&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides astonishing views of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Mauléon-Licharre?
A: While many routes in Mauléon-Licharre are rated moderate to difficult, the region does offer options for varying skill levels. For families, it's advisable to look for routes described as having mostly paved surfaces and less significant elevation gain. Always check the route details for specific difficulty and terrain before setting out.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while cycling?
A: The region is rich in history and culture. You can visit the imposing 12th-century Château de Mauléon, or the 17th-century Château d'Andurain de Maytie. The nearby village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6723006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets&lt;/a&gt; offers a lively central square. The town is also known as the capital of the espadrille, with workshops and an annual festival.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868821096/historic-loop-from-mauleon-licharre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic loop from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37637916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ahusquy Inn – Bridge over the Saison River loop from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route in the area?
A: One of the longer routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41078739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Saison River – Uhaïtxa loop from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 87.4 km and offers a demanding ride with significant elevation.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Osquich&lt;/a&gt; offers magnificent views of the Soule valley and the Pyrenees. Additionally, the Château de Mauléon provides a strategic viewpoint over the Saison Valley and distant mountains.

Q: What is the average rating for touring cycling routes in Mauléon-Licharre?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mauléon-Licharre are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.3 out of 5 stars from over 29 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes and challenging climbs.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Mauléon-Licharre and its surrounding villages, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6723006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets&lt;/a&gt;, offer various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. Many routes pass through or near these charming villages, providing opportunities for refreshments and rest.
tips: This ancient hospital foundation dates back to the 12th century and is located on the Pyrenean foothills of the Way of Saint James!

The memorial is a place to visit for what it suggests and evokes more than for what it shows because the remains of this 80 hectare internment camp opened from 1939 to 1945 are rare.

To borrow it is to remember!
